product.name: ArSuprem Static Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: 97% Cotton 3% Carbon Antistatic, Vat Dyed Single Jersey Fabric
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 97% Cotton 3% Carbon, Single Jersey, Antistatic, Vat Dyed Knit Fabric
descriptions: 97% cotton 3% Carbon single jersey, antisatic workwear knit fabric with very high chlorinated bleaching and light fastness.
ArSuprem Static Pro fabric are produced from the best quality combed yarn Turkish Cotton.
It is resistant to very high sun level, with fastness level of 6 to 7.
ArSuprem Static Pro fabrics can be bleached with peroxide and hypochloride without whitening in color.
Suitable for antistatic ESD application, certified for EN ISO 1149
Vat dyed ArSuprem Static Pro products can be used in the most hard working conditions and in military area.
97% Cotton 3% Carbon Antistatic, Vat Dyed Single Jersey Fabric
220 g/m², 97% Cotton 3% Carbon, Single Jersey, Antistatic, Vat Dyed Knit Fabric
technicalParameters: Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Fabric is antistatic (EN 1149, EN 61340) and ESD-certified, which are properties often relevant to electrical environments, but no explicit statement ties it to the energy sector or high-voltage work.
Product is explicitly categorized under the hi-vis category and source group for safety jackets, and lists hi-vis as a property, despite being a jersey knit rather than warp knit.
Antibacterial/antistatic finishes could theoretically support hospital-adjacent uses, but no medical or healthcare application is explicitly stated.
Explicit textual statement identifies suitability for military-area use, providing direct though brief evidence.

evidence: Suitable for antistatic ESD application, certified for EN ISO 1149
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly described as an antistatic ESD shirt fabric intended for general industrial protective workwear. Despite being catalogued under a hi-vis category source group, the technical data, application field and descriptions center on antistatic/ESD workwear rather than certified high-visibility warning use (no EN ISO 20471 or fluorescent colour evidence), so the most defensible primary use is general industrial workwear.

evidence: Vat dyed ArSuprem Static Pro products can be used in the most hard working conditions and in military area.
secondary.rationale: An explicit, though brief, statement ties the fabric to military-area use, providing independent support for a secondary military application distinct from the general workwear use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Category and source group labels reference hi-vis warp knit safety jackets, but the actual structure is a jersey knit and no certified high-visibility standard (e.g., EN ISO 20471) or fluorescent colour is stated; evidence instead centers on ESD/antistatic use, so hivis is not clearly supported as the dominant use.
Antistatic and ESD certification (EN 1149, EN 61340) alone do not establish an explicit electrical-utility or high-voltage application per rule 8/exclusion guidance.
No explicit medical or healthcare use stated; antistatic property alone is insufficient.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Category metadata (hi-vis warp knit safety jacket group) conflicts with the product's actual jersey knit structure and ESD-focused description, creating ambiguity about whether hi-vis certification is truly intended; human review recommended to confirm correct primary classification.
